title: W5 D Suggested Exercises
body:
Suggested Exercises for Week 5
                                   Juan Carlos Carbajal

                             ECON 3202 — Term 1, 2026
                                      UNSW Sydney


Problem 1. Let T1 and T2 be two linear functions mapping R n to R, and let α1 , α2 ∈ R be given.
Show that the function T defined on R n by

                                  T ( x) = α1 T1 ( x) + α2 T2 ( x)

is also linear.


Problem 2. In class we defined the standard basis for R2 as the collection {(1, 0), (0, 1)}. This
collection has two important (and defining) properties.
  (i) It is linear independent; i.e., the system of equations

                                     α1 (1, 0) + α2 (0, 1) = (0, 0)

      has as unique solution α1 = α2 = 0.
 (ii) It spans R2 ; i.e., any vector x = ( x1 , x2 ) in R2 can be expressed as a linear combination
      of (1, 0) and (0, 1):
                                     ( x1 , x2 ) = x1 (1, 0) + x2 (0, 1).

   As it turns out, the standard basis is not the only basis for R2 . There are plenty more.
Here’s one example: {(1, 1), (−1, 1)}. Show that this collection forms a basis for R2 ; i.e., it is
linearly independent and spans R2 .


Problem 3. This question asks you to characterize linear functions in Euclidean spaces. It can
be generalize to any finite dimensional linear vector space, but we will restrict to the more
familiar Euclidean spaces. Recall that if A is a p × q matrix, then At refers to the transpose of
A whose dimension is q × p.
  (a) Show that the function f : R m → R is linear if and only if there exists a vector a ∈ R m
      such that
                                 f ( x) = x · a,    for all x ∈ R m .

  (b) Show that the function g : R m → R n is linear if and only if there exists an m × n matrix
      A such that
                                 g( x)t = xt A,       for all x ∈ R m .



                                                 1
 Problem 4. Let f ( x ) = | x |. Show that f is not differentiable at x = 0.


Problem 5. In class we have seen the following result. Prove it.

   Proposition 1

   Let S ⊆ R be open. Assume that f : S → R has a derivative at x.

     (a) If D f ( x ) > 0, then there exists δ > 0 such that f is strictly increasing on ( x −
         δ, x + δ).

     (b) If D f ( x ) < 0, then there exists δ > 0 such that f is strictly decreasing on ( x −
         δ, x + δ).




Problem 6. Let a > 0 be a given constant. Show that the derivative of a x is

                                             Da x = a x ln a

Hint: Use the fact that a x = exp {ln a x } = exp { x ln a} and apply the chain rule.



Problem 7. Let n ∈ N with n ≥ 1, and define f : R → R by

                                               f ( x) = xn .

Using the definition of derivative, show by induction on n that

                                  D f ( x ) = nx n−1       for all x ∈ R.




                                                       2
